meta.name: Sonata
meta.release_type: Documentation update — patch over v0.5.0 initial release
meta.status: Feasibility study — no Vial configuration yet.
meta.tagline: 28 keys. Cadence's layer architecture, half a column lighter.
meta.author: one7two99
meta.documentation_scope: This file specifies the Sonata Base Layer only. All other layer contents (L1 through L12) are inherited 1:1 from Cadence v1.11.1. See https://github.com/one7two99/cadence for the full layer reference.

design_philosophy.origin: Sonata is a feasibility study built around two strict ergonomic constraints: (1) no lateral inward movement of the index fingers (no inner column), (2) thumbs only move outward, never inward. Both constraints proved viable on a 28-key layout.
contributions_to_cadence: L1 Overflow + International concept: the design pattern of putting low-frequency letters (X, Q) plus international characters (umlauts, ß, €) on a single thumb-activated layer originated in Sonata's overflow-letter problem.

L12 Symbol layer redesign: Sonata's tighter constraints (4 columns instead of 5) forced a frequency-and-position-quality based symbol layout that Cadence then adopted. The L12 specification is shared between Sonata and Cadence.
Validation of thumb-outward-only principle: Cadence's thumb-trigger conventions (Spc tap+hold for Mouse, Tab-hold for Overflow) honour Sonata's outward-only thumb constraint.
design_philosophy.relationship_to_cadence: Sonata inherits all layer contents from Cadence v1.11.1. The only Sonata-specific element is the Base Layer alpha grid, optimised for SFB on the 28-key constraint. Layer triggers occupy the same physical positions as in Cadence — same finger, same row — so muscle memory transfers without retraining when switching between hardware.
hardware_options: Ferris Sweep with the inner column left unprogrammed (the inner column keys exist physically but carry KC_NO)

physical_layout.structure: 4 finger columns × 3 rows × 2 sides + 2 thumbs × 2 sides
physical_layout.missing_relative_to_cadence: the inner column (5th column on each side) — present in Cadence as G/M plus B/V/J/K, absent in Sonata

diagram_ascii: Z W G M | L Y P J
A R S T | N E I O
V C U D | H F B K

overflow_letters_on_L1.X: R_ring_home (I-position) on L1 — same as Cadence
overflow_letters_on_L1.Q: R_mid_home (E-position) on L1 — same as Cadence

home_row_mods.notation: Each home-row position is a Tap Dance: tap = letter, hold = modifier
home_row_mods.design_notes: Identical to Cadence v1.11.1. Bilateral Meta+Alt is cross-hand (A-hold + I-hold or O-hold + R-hold), avoiding same-hand HRM conflicts. Tipping terms 250ms for ring/pinky and 200ms for mid/index reflect per-finger speed differences.

layer_triggers.notation: Position-based mapping. Triggers occupy the same physical positions as in Cadence v1.11.1 — same finger, same row — even though the letter on that position differs from Cadence's grid. This preserves muscle memory across hardware.

L_thumb_outer (Spc).notes: Spc tap+hold pattern carried over from Cadence v1.11. Resolves Home Row Mod blocking that occurs with bilateral letter-pair triggers for Mouse.

L_thumb_inner (Tab).notes: Tab as Tap-Dance carrier carried over from Cadence v1.11. International is the most-used special-character layer when writing German prose; thumb-hold gives maximum accessibility.

bilateral_letter_triggers.notation: Hold either of the two letters listed below to activate the layer. Triggers are finger-symmetric: same finger on both hands, same row.

L6_Fn_Media.description: Hold G or Hold Y to activate L6 Fn + Media.

L7_Code_CLI.description: Hold W or Hold P to activate L7 Code & CLI.

L8_Tiling_WM.description: Hold V or Hold K to activate L8 Tiling WM.

L9_Brackets.description: Hold C or Hold B to activate L9 Brackets. App/Menu on L9 Spc-thumb and L9 Bsp-thumb (inherited from Cadence v1.11.1).

L11_Firmware_Control.description: Long-hold (500ms) Z or J to activate L11 Firmware Control. Deliberately uncomfortable to prevent accidental QK_BOOT or QK_REBOOT triggering.

layers_without_trigger.L2: Deprecated in Cadence v1.11.1 (replaced by L12). Sonata mirrors this — L2 has no trigger.
layers_without_trigger.L10: Clipboard layer in Cadence has no trigger by user choice. Sonata mirrors this.

layer_inheritance.notation: All layer contents (what each key produces while a layer is active) are inherited 1:1 from Cadence v1.11.1. This specification does not duplicate that information.
layer_inheritance.source_of_truth: https://github.com/one7two99/cadence — see docs/index.html for the complete layer reference.

layers_inherited.L1: Overflow + International — umlauts, ß, €, dead-key fallback, X and Q overflow letters
layers_inherited.L6: Fn + Media — F1-F12 plus media controls
layers_inherited.L7: Code & CLI — shell operators, path navigation, expansion macros
layers_inherited.L8: Tiling WM — workspaces 1-10, focus, window movement
layers_inherited.L9: Brackets — bilateral mirror of bracket pairs as Tap Dance, App/Menu on outer thumbs
layers_inherited.L10: Clipboard — present in firmware, no trigger by user choice
layers_inherited.L11: Firmware Control — QK_BOOT and QK_REBOOT, safety-gated by long-hold
layers_inherited.L12: Symbols — frequency-and-strength symbol layout, shared specification with Cadence

layer_inheritance.hardware_constraints_inherited: Cadence's layer contents are designed to leave the inner column (G/M/B/V/J/K positions) free of layer content. This makes them directly compatible with Sonata's 28-key grid where the inner column does not exist physically.

versioning_policy.current: v0.5.1 — documentation update over v0.5.0 initial release

scheme.documentation_update: Patch level — v0.5.0 → v0.5.1 (text, diagrams, fixes)
scheme.configuration_change: Minor level — v0.5.0 → v0.6.0 (Base Layer, triggers, HRM)

versioning_policy.v0_5_0_release: Initial public release of the Sonata specification
versioning_policy.v0_5_1_release: Documentation update — Base Layer keyboard visualisation in index.html now correctly places thumb keys (Spc/Tab on the left, Ent/Bsp on the right) below the index columns, matching physical Ferris Sweep geometry.
versioning_policy.next_milestone: v1.0 — tagged when a dedicated Vial configuration exists. From v1.0 onward, documentation will track two version numbers: the Sonata version (Base Layer state) and the inherited Cadence version.

compatibility_notes.muscle_memory_transfer: A user proficient with Cadence on Ferris Sweep can move to Sonata hardware (or run Sonata on Sweep with unprogrammed inner column) without retraining the layer-trigger fingers. The same physical position activates the same layer. Only the alpha grid differs — the seven letters that occupy off-home positions on Cadence's outer column (Q, P, B / J, ', K) are not present on Sonata's base, but they are reachable on L1 Overflow (X, Q) or remain in their alpha positions for letters that fit Sonata's 4-column grid.
compatibility_notes.running_sonata_on_sweep: On Ferris Sweep hardware, the inner column physical keys (5th column each side) carry KC_NO — they are present but do nothing. The remaining 28 keys behave identically to a dedicated 28-key build.
compatibility_notes.not_a_strict_subset_of_cadence: Sonata's alpha grid (Z W G M / L Y P J on top, V C U D / H F B K on bottom) is the result of independent SFB optimisation under the no-pinky-column constraint. It is not derivable by removing letters from Cadence's grid — several letters are repositioned to columns they don't occupy in Cadence.
